# Nodes & Validators

This hub helps you choose between being a validator, hosting your own RPC endpoint or contributing prover capacity to the zero-knowledge sync.

<u-page-grid>
<u-page-card description="Deploy a full or history node that matches your infrastructure and security needs." icon="i-tabler:server-bolt" title="Run a Node" to="https://github.com/nimiq/core-rs-albatross?tab=readme-ov-file#configuration" variant="outline">



</u-page-card>

<u-page-card description="Meet staking requirements, configure keys, and maintain uptime to earn rewards for securing the network." icon="i-nimiq:verified" title="Become a Validator" to="./validators/becoming-a-validator" variant="outline">



</u-page-card>

<u-page-card description="Supply zk-SNARK proofs for Albatross with the hardware, storage, and monitoring it demands." icon="i-tabler:cpu" title="Operate a Prover" to="./prover-node-guide" variant="outline">



</u-page-card>
</u-page-grid>

## Helpful references

- [Web Client vs RPC Client](/web-client/web-client-vs-rpc) — decide when a hosted node is necessary.
- [JSON-RPC Methods](../rpc/methods/) — integrate your applications once the endpoint is live.
- [Protocol documentation](/protocol/) — understand how validators, staking, and ZKP systems fit into Albatross.
